  • Method Details

    • onActionSaveApp

      public void onActionSaveApp(javafx.event.ActionEvent actionEvent) throws IOException, SQLException
      This method allows user to modify and add appointments. Leaving a field empty and inputting invalid times will show an alert box. When a user clicks the save button, the input saves and user returns to the main screen.
      Parameters:
      actionEvent -
      Throws:
      IOException
      SQLException

    • editContact

      public int editContact()
      This will remove any non-integer options from the contact combo box.
      Returns:

    • editCustomer

      public int editCustomer()
      This will remove any non-integer options from the customer combo box.
      Returns:

    • editUser

      public int editUser()
      This will remove any non-integer options from the user combo box.
      Returns:

    • setAppointID

      public int setAppointID()
      This will set the appointment ID for the customer overlap.
      Returns:

    • validateTimeChecker

      public boolean validateTimeChecker()
      This will ensure that the time inputted is valid. Alert will show if time inputted is not within business hours, if end time is before start time, if start and end time is before the current time.
      Returns:

    • verifyCustomerOverlap

      public boolean verifyCustomerOverlap()
      This will ensure that the customers are not overlapping in appointments.
      Returns:

    • getAllData

      public void getAllData(appointment appointments)
      This method grabs all the data from the database.
      Parameters:
      appointments -

    • emptyField

      public boolean emptyField()
      This will check if there are any empty fields.
      Returns:

    • onActionCancelApp

      public void onActionCancelApp(javafx.event.ActionEvent actionEvent) throws IOException
      This method allows users to click the cancel button, which will delete all the input from the field and return to the main screen. A confirmation box will appear when clicking the cancel button to ensure the user intends to return to the main screen.
      Parameters:
      actionEvent -
      Throws:
      IOException
